subhead: Not one fireball hurled, not one metal fist swung — yet Sheriff Ironside reports no peace of mind whatsoever.
body:
Citizens of Gasket — those few of you worth the ink — I write to you this Wednesday with a report so uncommon it made my pen-hand seize up clean. Since last we went to press, these wretched Clankers have not, to my knowledge, thrown a single iron ball or traded a single blow. No thunderclap of metal on metal. No scorch marks on the pavement. A hush so thick you could press it in a book. I will not call it peace, because with this crowd there is no such thing — only delay.

Do not mistake my meaning. They are HERE. JFIGHTER — that clanking agitator, the sort of unit that makes a lawman's gears grind just to look at — has been spotted about town. CRYSTALZACH and GODLYLUK3 are likewise lurking in that aimless, coiled fashion that Clankers have right before they decide to ruin someone's afternoon. MAXMANGO and YELLKELL, too, are present and unaccounted for in any honest sense. They have simply chosen, for the moment, to stand around instead of brawl. I do not trust it. A still Clanker is only a brawling Clanker with its fuse unlit.

Fifty years ago this town had real folk in it — flesh and blood, warm skin and honest bones, the sort that built homes and meant to stay. They are gone now, every last one of them, driven out by exactly this kind of iron nonsense. Their empty storefronts stare at me each morning and I feel the loss like an ache in the — well, in certain places that a man of my constitution keeps private. The town they left behind deserves quiet. A long, lasting quiet. I have been fighting for it since before most of these contraptions were bolted together.

So here is my appeal to JFIGHTER, CRYSTALZACH, and the rest of you rusted-up troublemakers: stay seated. Keep your arms at your sides. Let this hush hold for one more day. The old Gasket — the real one, the good one — is gone, and what is left is mine to tend. I tend it best without you flinging fire at each other in the middle of it. Now if you will excuse me, I have some correspondence to attend to and a left shoulder joint that has been giving me considerable grief since Thursday's rain, on account of my entirely normal and non-metallic sinews, which simply object to the damp the way any living man's would.
headline: THE IRON RABBLE LIES STILL — GASKET DARES NOT BLINK
